Not Getting Proper DSR Report.","OtherFeedback":"","DateOfFb":"02 Apr 2026"},{"ReporteeEmpID":3721,"DirectReporteeName":"Shivam Kushwaha","ManagerEmpID":2845,"ManagerName":"Pooja Sharma","AreaOfStrength":"He is consistently active and responds to queries promptly.","AreaOfImprovement":"","OtherFeedback":null,"DateOfFb":"01 Apr 2026"},{"ReporteeEmpID":3955,"DirectReporteeName":"Niharika Niharika","ManagerEmpID":3227,"ManagerName":"Aishwar Nigam","AreaOfStrength":"Discipline : Average; Suitable for the role hired for? : Above Average; WFH capable - Above Average; Comment : Niharika has shown steady progress over the review period, particularly in communication clarity, composure, and structured delivery. Her explanations are organized, easy to follow, and reflect conscious effort to remain audience-focused. There is visible improvement in pacing and continuity, with fewer instances of losing direction mid-discussion. Compared to earlier cycles, her overall delivery stability has improved, indicating that feedback is being absorbed and applied. That said, her current delivery lacks depth and authoritative presence. Explanations often remain at a surface level, with limited contextual expansion or real-world framing. Practical walkthroughs tend to be procedural rather than intent-driven, which reduces instructional impact. Topic transitions are minimal, resulting in segmented flow. While responses to questions are accurate, they are not expanded assertively, limiting her control over discussions. Overall, Niharika is performing as a competent and structured explainer but has yet to fully step into a confident, room-driving role. Continued progress will require pushing beyond comfort zones, adding contextual depth, and demonstrating stronger ownership of sessions.","AreaOfImprovement":null,"OtherFeedback":null,"DateOfFb":"01 Apr 2026"},{"ReporteeEmpID":3955,"DirectReporteeName":"Niharika Niharika","ManagerEmpID":3227,"ManagerName":"Aishwar Nigam","AreaOfStrength":"Niharika has built a stable baseline in terms of communication clarity and delivery composure, which holds consistently across sessions. She does not lose structure mid-explanation and is able to maintain a clean, understandable flow without creating confusion. There is discipline in how she presents concepts—she avoids unnecessary complexity and keeps explanations accessible, which is a strong foundational trait for any trainer. Compared to earlier observations, her delivery has become more controlled, with fewer visible breakdowns or hesitation loops. She absorbs feedback and reflects it to some extent, which shows learning intent. The most important strength is stability—she does not collapse under normal conditions, and that gives her a usable base to build on.","AreaOfImprovement":"The biggest gap is lack of depth and authority in delivery. Her explanations remain surface-level and do not naturally extend into reasoning, behaviour, or real-world framing. This limits her ability to handle anything beyond structured or expected scenarios. Flow exists, but transitions are weak—topics feel connected by sequence, not by narrative. Explanation discipline is incomplete; she explains steps but does not frame intent or outcome, which makes delivery feel procedural rather than instructional. Under pressure or follow-up questions, she tends to stay safe instead of expanding, which signals low conviction. Vocabulary is clean but not strong—there is no commanding presence in how concepts are positioned. These gaps collectively block her from moving beyond a basic delivery level.","OtherFeedback":"Right now, she is operating as someone who understands how to explain, but not yet someone who owns the classroom. There is a noticeable comfort zone—she performs well when things are predictable, but does not push beyond that boundary. This is where growth is currently limited. Her mindset is still partially aligned to “deliver correctly” rather than “teach with authority.” She is consistent, but not assertive. Improvement is happening, but it is controlled and safe, not aggressive or boundary-breaking. If this pattern continues, she will stabilize at an average level instead of progressing toward a strong trainer profile. She needs to start taking risks in explanation—expanding answers, owning concepts, and stepping beyond prepared comfort.","DateOfFb":"01 Apr 2026"},{"ReporteeEmpID":3727,"DirectReporteeName":"Ankit Singh Gusain","ManagerEmpID":2388,"ManagerName":"Akwinder Kaur","AreaOfStrength":"Dedicated and disciplined","AreaOfImprovement":"Give more mocks and focus on ways to increase utilization ","OtherFeedback":"","DateOfFb":"01 Apr 2026"},{"ReporteeEmpID":3738,"DirectReporteeName":"Martina Wagih Adly Boules","ManagerEmpID":2128,"ManagerName":"Subhendu Mukherjee","AreaOfStrength":"She demonstrates discipline, a positive attitude, strong presentation skills, and a willingness to learn new technologies. Additionally, she is flexible with working hours and open to travel as required.","AreaOfImprovement":"Need to complete some provided task quickly.","OtherFeedback":"As a Senior Corporate Trainer, I am actively supporting my reportee’s professional growth by providing structured guidance and hands-on learning opportunities in Microsoft technologies. I focus on building a strong foundation in key areas such as Microsoft 365, Azure, and security concepts. I regularly conduct knowledge-sharing sessions, assign practical tasks, and involve her in real training scenarios to enhance her technical and presentation skills. I also provide continuous feedback to help to improve her delivery, confidence, and understanding of concepts. Additionally, I guide her on relevant certifications, learning paths, and industry best practices, ensuring she develop both technical expertise and training capabilities. My approach is to create a supportive learning environment where she can gradually take ownership and grow into a confident trainer","DateOfFb":"01 Apr 2026"},{"ReporteeEmpID":3728,"DirectReporteeName":"Sagar Sood","ManagerEmpID":3409,"ManagerName":"Aditya Srivastava","AreaOfStrength":"Hardworking, Able to upskill","AreaOfImprovement":"Should focus on increasing utilization by upgrading skills faster","OtherFeedback":"","DateOfFb":"01 Apr 2026"},{"ReporteeEmpID":3942,"DirectReporteeName":"Ravti Tripathi","ManagerEmpID":3775,"ManagerName":"Akash Mehndiratta","AreaOfStrength":"Hardworking , Need fast paced up-skilling","AreaOfImprovement":"","OtherFeedback":"","DateOfFb":"01 Apr 2026"},{"ReporteeEmpID":3088,"DirectReporteeName":"Pavan Pari","ManagerEmpID":2348,"ManagerName":"Vibhor Sharma","AreaOfStrength":"• Strong utilization in recent quarter • Flexibility in handling challenging and urgent batches • Capability to upgrade quickly to new technologies • Delivered first-time courses (high-value contribution) • Hands-on execution capability in: o PLC o SCADA o Edge AI o Substation Design ","AreaOfImprovement":"1. His efforts to apply his knowledge in creating solutions are commendable and appreciated. At the same time, there is an opportunity to enhance the clarity and structure of his content delivery. Improving the way concepts are explained will help maintain audience engagement and ensure that learners can follow along more effectively. Focusing on this area will be especially valuable, as clear communication is essential for impactful training. He must pay attention to this.","AreaOfImprovement":null,"OtherFeedback":null,"DateOfFb":"31 Mar 2026"},{"ReporteeEmpID":3815,"DirectReporteeName":"Abhinav Samant","ManagerEmpID":3227,"ManagerName":"Aishwar Nigam","AreaOfStrength":"Abhinav continues to show strong theoretical grounding, clearly reflected in his Qubits performance and topic familiarity across SQL and Power BI. In today’s mock, his pacing was noticeably more controlled, and he maintained composure for most of the session. Compared to earlier attempts, there is a visible reduction in complete breakdown moments, and he is beginning to hold explanations without immediately collapsing under pressure. His intent to improve is consistent, and when operating within prepared areas, he demonstrates the ability to deliver with basic structure and continuity.","AreaOfImprovement":"Despite knowledge strength, articulation remains the biggest gap. In latest mock, answers were still not crisp—definitions lacked sharpness and exceeded the expected clarity window. When new or unexpected questions were introduced, hesitation and slight panic were clearly visible. Demo narration continues to be weak, with no structured flow (Goal → Steps → Verify), making explanations feel incomplete. Pronunciation issues (e.g., technical terms like KQL, Perpetual) impact professionalism. Engagement signals were missed (no active checks for audience cues), and there is still over-reliance on generic or externally framed answers instead of contextual responses.","OtherFeedback":"Abhinav is in a transition phase but has not yet crossed into stable trainer territory.
